title: Rig project profile
description: Per-project configuration read by rig skills at runtime. Lives at .rig/config.json in the consuming project. Every parameterizable skill reads this instead of hardcoding project specifics. Missing keys fall back to the documented defaults.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema for editor validation.

name.description: Human name of the project, e.g. "Acme".

repo.description: GitHub owner/name, e.g. "my-org/my-app". Used by review/CI skills for gh api calls.

runtime.description: Language toolchain. Drives install/test/build command generation and CI setup steps.

packageManager.description: Also selects the CI setup action (setup-bun vs setup-node) and install command.

installCommand.description: Override the derived install command (default derived from packageManager, e.g. "bun install").

test.description: How to run tests. Consumed by rig-debug, rig-tidy, rig-review, rig-worktree, rig-task, and the CI test-gate.

command.description: Full unit-test suite command, e.g. "bun run test".

requiresDatabase.description: If true, the CI test-gate provisions a throwaway Postgres service.

sourceScope.description: Default path globs skills operate on when none is given, e.g. ["packages/api/src"]. rig-tidy/rig-spike/rig-sprint use the first entry as their default scope.

vcs.description: Branch and base-ref conventions. Consumed by rig-worktree, rig-task, rig-review, rig-sprint.

baseRef.description: What rig-review diffs against and worktrees branch from.

branchConvention.description: Template for new branch names. Placeholders: {user}, {ticket}, {slug}.

protectedBranchMergeQueue.description: If true, flows use `gh pr merge --auto` and never pass --rebase/--squash (the queue's method wins).

tracker.description: Issue tracker binding. Consumed by ticket, sprint, and implement-style flows. Set provider to "none" to strip all tracker steps.

team.description: Linear team name/key (e.g. "Engineering") or GitHub org.

ticketPrefix.description: Ticket identifier prefix, e.g. "INC-". Used to detect ticket IDs in branch names and PR titles.

labelMapFile.description: Path to the PR/tracker label source-of-truth doc.

githubIntegration.description: If true, the tracker auto-transitions issue state from GitHub branch/PR events, so skills must NOT set states manually.

review.description: Code-review configuration. Consumed by rig-review (find and fix verbs).

patternsFile.description: Path to the project's P0–P3 review-pattern catalog.

bot.description: Which PR review bot to poll/re-trigger in rig-review fix, if any.

botRetrigger.description: The comment string that re-triggers the bot, e.g. "@codex review".

maxRounds.description: Max fix<->re-review rounds before handing back to a human.

agents.description: Optional overrides mapping the kit's canonical agent roles to the agent names registered in this project. Defaults to the kit's own agents, rig-<role> (e.g. debugger->rig-debugger). Set a value only to point a role at a differently-named agent.

ci.description: CI-template parameters. Only relevant if you installed workflows from ci/. Each installed workflow documents which of these it reads.

imageRegistry.description: Container registry for the generic image-build workflow.

slackWebhookSecret.description: Secret name holding the CI Slack webhook, consumed by slack-notify.

trustBoundaryPaths.description: Paths that trip the deeper security-scan gate (auth, secrets, tenancy, billing surfaces).
